bio: Second son of Jacob and Leah. He and Levi massacred the men of Shechem to avenge their sister Dinah. Jacob prophesied that his tribe would be scattered in Israel. His tribe was eventually absorbed into the territory of Judah.

name: Dan
bio: Fifth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah (Rachel's handmaid). His tribe settled in the far north of Israel. The city of Dan became one of the places where Jeroboam set up golden calves.

name: Gad
bio: Seventh son of Jacob, born to Zilpah (Leah's handmaid). His tribe settled east of the Jordan River. Jacob prophesied that a troop would overcome him, but he would overcome at the last.

name: Asher
bio: Eighth son of Jacob, born to Zilpah (Leah's handmaid). His tribe was promised rich food and royal delicacies. Anna the prophetess in the New Testament was of the tribe of Asher.

name: Naphtali
bio: Sixth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah (Rachel's handmaid). His tribe settled in the region of Galilee, where much of Jesus' ministry would later occur.

name: Issachar
bio: Ninth son of Jacob and Leah. His tribe was known for understanding the times and knowing what Israel ought to do.

name: Zebulun
bio: Tenth son of Jacob and Leah. Jacob prophesied that Zebulun would dwell at the haven of the sea and be a haven for ships.

name: Anna
bio: An elderly prophetess of the tribe of Asher who spent her days fasting and praying in the temple. She was present when the infant Jesus was brought to the temple and spoke of him to all who looked for the redemption of Jerusalem.

name: Simeon
bio: A devout man in Jerusalem who was promised by the Holy Ghost that he would not die before seeing the Messiah. When Mary and Joseph brought the infant Jesus to the temple, Simeon took the child in his arms and prophesied, including that a sword would pierce Mary's soul.

name: Herodias
bio: Wife of Herod Antipas and granddaughter of Herod the Great. She left her first husband Philip to marry Antipas, which John the Baptist condemned. She orchestrated John's beheading by having her daughter Salome request his head as a reward for her dance.

name: Zenos
bio: An Israelite prophet quoted in the Book of Mormon whose writings were on the brass plates but are not in the current Bible. He authored the allegory of the olive tree (Jacob 5), one of the longest chapters in the Book of Mormon. He also prophesied of Christ's crucifixion and the signs that would attend it.

name: Zenock
bio: An Israelite prophet quoted in the Book of Mormon whose writings were on the brass plates. He prophesied of Christ's coming and was slain for his testimony. He is mentioned alongside Zenos and Neum as brass plate prophets not found in the current Bible.

name: Neum
bio: An Israelite prophet mentioned in 1 Nephi 19:10 whose writings were on the brass plates. He testified that the God of Israel would be crucified. His writings are not found in the current Bible.

name: Cezoram
bio: A Nephite chief judge who was murdered by secret combination members. His son was also killed while serving as chief judge. Their murders marked a period of increasing Gadianton influence in Nephite government.

name: Nephihah
bio: The second chief judge of the Nephites, appointed by Alma the Younger when he stepped down to preach. Nephihah was a just man who served faithfully until his death. He was succeeded by his son Pahoran.

name: Morianton
bio: A Nephite leader who tried to lead his people to the land northward after a dispute. Captain Moroni sent Teancum to intercept him, and he was killed. He had been a violent man who beat one of his female servants.

name: Coriantumr
bio: A descendant of Zarahemla who, as a Lamanite military commander, led a bold attack straight through Nephite territory to capture Zarahemla. He was eventually surrounded and forced to surrender.

name: Lehi
bio: A Nephite military commander who served under Captain Moroni during the wars with the Lamanites. He was given command of the city of Mulek and helped in several key battles. He should not be confused with the prophet Lehi, father of Nephi.

name: Jesse
bio: Father of King David and grandson of Ruth and Boaz. He was a shepherd in Bethlehem. Isaiah prophesied that a rod would come forth from the stem of Jesse, understood as a Messianic prophecy.

name: Nathan
bio: A prophet who served in the courts of David and Solomon. He confronted David about his sin with Bathsheba using the parable of the ewe lamb, declaring, "Thou art the man." He also helped ensure Solomon's succession to the throne.

name: Abigail
bio: The intelligent and beautiful wife of the wealthy but churlish Nabal. When Nabal refused David's request for provisions, Abigail intervened with gifts and wise words, preventing David from shedding blood in anger. After Nabal died, David married her.

name: Elkanah
bio: Father of the prophet Samuel and husband of Hannah and Peninnah. He was a devout man who made yearly pilgrimages to worship at Shiloh. He supported Hannah when she dedicated their son Samuel to the Lord's service.

name: Joab
bio: David's nephew and commander of his army. He was a fierce and effective military leader who captured Jerusalem and won many battles. He killed Absalom against David's wishes and later supported Adonijah's claim to the throne, for which Solomon had him executed.

name: Adonijah
bio: Fourth son of David who attempted to seize the throne as David lay dying. His claim was thwarted by Nathan, Bathsheba, and Zadok, who secured Solomon's coronation. He was later executed by Solomon after requesting David's concubine Abishag as a wife.

name: Michal
bio: Daughter of King Saul and first wife of David. She helped David escape from Saul by lowering him through a window. Later, she despised David for dancing before the Ark of the Covenant and was told she would have no children.

name: Uriah
bio: A loyal soldier in David's army and husband of Bathsheba. After David committed adultery with Bathsheba, he arranged for Uriah to be placed in the front lines of battle where he would be killed. Uriah's unwitting faithfulness (he refused to go home to his wife while his comrades were fighting) contrasts sharply with David's treachery.

name: Haman
bio: A high official in the Persian court of King Ahasuerus who plotted to destroy all the Jews because Mordecai refused to bow to him. His plot was exposed by Queen Esther, and he was hanged on the gallows he had prepared for Mordecai.

name: Cyrus
bio: King of Persia who conquered Babylon and issued a decree allowing the Jews to return to Jerusalem and rebuild the temple. Isaiah prophesied of him by name about 150 years before his birth, calling him God's anointed (Isaiah 44:28–45:1).

name: Delilah
bio: A woman in the Valley of Sorek who was bribed by the Philistine lords to discover the source of Samson's strength. She persistently pressed Samson until he revealed that his strength came from his uncut hair. She had his hair cut while he slept, leading to his capture.

name: Barak
bio: An Israelite military commander whom the prophetess Deborah directed to lead 10,000 men against the Canaanite general Sisera. He agreed to go only if Deborah accompanied him. The victory was decisive, but the honor of killing Sisera went to Jael.

name: Jael
bio: Wife of Heber the Kenite who killed the Canaanite general Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple while he slept in her tent. She is celebrated in the Song of Deborah as "blessed above women."

name: Pharaoh
bio: The Egyptian ruler during the Exodus whose heart was hardened against releasing the Israelites. Despite ten devastating plagues, he refused until the death of the firstborn. He pursued the Israelites to the Red Sea, where his army was destroyed. His exact identity is debated by historians.

name: John Murdock
bio: An early convert whose wife died giving birth to twins. The twins were adopted by Joseph and Emma Smith (who had recently lost twins of their own). He received a revelation in D&C 99 calling him to preach.

name: Jared Carter
bio: An early Church member who received a revelation (D&C 79) calling him to preach the gospel. He served several missions and was known for his effective proselytizing.

name: Stephen Burnett
bio: An early Church member called on a mission in D&C 80. He later left the Church during the Kirtland apostasy of 1838.
